title: podman generate kube doesn't export Entrypoint
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

when creating a container with a different entrypoint than in the pulled image, the generated kube yaml doesn't set the proper Command

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman container create --name foobar --replace  --entrypoint=/bin/zsh registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed:latest

2. podman  generate kube foobar

**Describe the results you received:**

# Generation of Kubernetes YAML is still under development!
#
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-2.2.0
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2021-01-06T14:40:53Z"
  labels:
    app: foobar
  name: foobar
spec:
  containers:
  - env:
    - name: PATH
      value: /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
    - name: TERM
      value: xterm
    - name: container
      value: podman
    - name: HOSTNAME
    image: registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed:latest
    name: foobar
    resources: {}
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
      capabilities: {}
      privileged: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
      seLinuxOptions: {}
    workingDir: /
status: {}
---
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
spec: {}
status:
  loadBalancer: {}

**Describe the results you expected:**
# Generation of Kubernetes YAML is still under development!
#
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-2.2.0
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2021-01-06T14:40:53Z"
  labels:
    app: foobar
  name: foobar
spec:
  containers:
  - env:
    - name: PATH
      value: /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
    - name: TERM
      value: xterm
    - name: container
      value: podman
    - name: HOSTNAME
    image: registry.opensuse.org/opensuse/tumbleweed:latest
    name: foobar
    command: ["/bin/zsh"]
    resources: {}
    securityContext:
      allowPrivilegeEscalation: true
      capabilities: {}
      privileged: false
      readOnlyRootFilesystem: false
      seLinuxOptions: {}
    workingDir: /
status: {}
---
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: null
spec: {}
status:
  loadBalancer: {}


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

Version:      2.2.0
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.13.15
Built:        Wed Dec  2 01:00:00 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

comments.body:
use the latest code:
```
 ./bin/podman pod create --name t2
./bin/podman container create --pod t2 --entrypoint top $IMG
./bin/podman generate kube t2
# Generation of Kubernetes YAML is still under development!
#
# Save the output of this file and use kubectl create -f to import
# it into Kubernetes.
#
# Created with podman-3.0.0-dev
apiVersion: v1
kind: Pod
metadata:
  creationTimestamp: "2021-01-07T02:22:05Z"
  labels:
    app: t2
  name: t2
spec:
  containers:
  - command:
    - top  <------------------ see this -----------------------------------------------
...

```
/close

@zhangguanzhang: Closing this issue.

<details>

In response to [this](https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/8898#issuecomment-755855521):

>./bin/podman generate kube t2
># Generation of Kubernetes YAML is still under development!

># Created with podman-3.0.0-dev
>apiVersion: v1

>  labels:

>  name: t2
>spec:
>  containers:
>  - command:
>    - top  <------------------ see this
>    env:
>    - name: PATH
>      value: /usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin
>    - name: TERM
>      value: xterm
>    - name: container
>      value: podman
>    - name: HOSTNAME
>      value: t2
>    image: docker.io/library/alpine:latest
>    name: xenodochialishizaka
>    resources: {}
>    securityContext:
>
>```
>/close


Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>
